item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish court annuls main opposition party congress in blow to CHP
item.description: An Ankara court suspended the leadership of Turkey's main opposition CHP, which defeated Erdogan's AKP in 2024 local elections. The ruling removes the party that won major victories in Istanbul and Ankara from power.

item.title: 🇲🇾 Malaysia demands TikTok explain AI deepfake account insulting king
item.description: Malaysia's government summoned TikTok over a fake account using AI to create offensive content about the king, including false claims about eating pork and images with his face on animal bodies.

item.title: 🇹🇭 Thai princess Bajrakitiyabha health declines amid multiple infections
item.description: The medical condition of Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha has worsened due to multiple infections affecting several organs. The 44-year-old princess has been hospitalized since December 2022 after suffering cardiac arrest.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇳 US seeks major expansion of energy exports to India, says Rubio
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced the US wants to significantly expand energy exports to India amid the Iran crisis disrupting traditional supply routes. The move aims to strengthen US-India energy security ties.

item.title: 🇪🇪🇺🇦 NATO jet downs suspected stray Ukrainian drone over Estonia
item.description: A NATO fighter jet intercepted and shot down a suspected Ukrainian drone that strayed into Estonian airspace. The incident highlights growing risks as Ukraine escalates long-range drone strikes on Russian infrastructure.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan Mediates as Iran Hardens Stance on Uranium in Peace Talks
item.description: Pakistan intensified diplomacy to advance US-Iran peace negotiations, but Tehran appeared to harden its position on nuclear materials. Trump warned of military action if he does not get the right answers, while Iran reviews the latest US proposal.

item.title: ☢️ Iran Supreme Leader Orders Enriched Uranium to Stay in Country
item.description: Ayatollah Khamenei issued a directive that Iran near-weapons-grade uranium must not be sent abroad, a hardline stance against a key US demand. The announcement caused oil prices and Treasury yields to rise while US stocks dipped.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇷🇺 Kremlin Dismisses Report of China Training Russian Soldiers for Ukraine
item.description: The Kremlin rejected as false a Reuters report that China's army secretly trained about 200 Russian soldiers in China late last year, some of whom went on to fight in Ukraine. The accusation adds to Western concerns about deepening China-Russia military ties.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Diplomacy Unravels Under Trump, Reshaping Global Alliances
item.description: A Reuters investigation finds that Trump's threats, personal envoys and hollowed-out embassies are reshaping US diplomacy. Allies from Europe to Asia are forging new channels as decisions increasingly driven by personalities, not institutions.

item.title: 🇯🇵💴 Bank of Japan Calls for Rate Hike as War-Fueled Inflation Looms
item.description: BOJ board member Junko Koeda urged interest rate hikes at an appropriate pace, warning that Middle East war price pressures could push underlying inflation above the 2 percent target. The comments bolstered expectations for a June rate increase.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇮🇷 Germany Charges Two with Iran-Backed Murder Plot Against Pro-Israel Figure
item.description: German prosecutors charged a Danish national and his Afghan accomplice with planning to kill a prominent pro-Israel public figure and spying on Jews for Iranian intelligence. The case highlights Iran's continued covert operations in Europe.

item.title: 🇪🇹🇪🇬🌊 Ethiopia Accuses Egypt of Obstructing Red Sea Access Bid
item.description: Ethiopia accused Egypt of attempting to undermine its efforts to secure Red Sea access, days after Cairo signed a maritime cooperation agreement with Eritrea. The dispute adds to regional tensions over strategic waterways and access.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇮🇱⚓ UK Summons Israeli Diplomat Over Gaza Flotilla Activist Treatment
item.description: The UK summoned Israel's top diplomat after far-right minister Ben Gvir posted a video taunting detained pro-Palestinian activists. Hundreds of flotilla activists have been deported following international backlash over their treatment.

item.title: 🏛️🇨🇺🇺🇸 US Supreme Court Rules for Cuban Property Owners in Castro-Era Case
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled 8-1 in favor of Havana Docks, reviving claims against cruise lines that used property confiscated during the Cuban revolution. The decision allows owners of seized Cuban property to pursue compensation.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Deports 430 Foreign Flotilla Activists After Ben Gvir Controversy
item.description: Israel has deported all 430 foreign activists from the Gaza-bound flotilla, with Turkey sending planes to collect them. Italy has requested EU sanctions on far-right minister Itamar Ben Gvir over his handling of detained activists, while Poland considers banning him.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese Supertankers Exit Hormuz as Iran Conflict Shows Signs of Thaw
item.description: Two Chinese supertankers were among a group of vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, suggesting a possible de-escalation in the US-Iran standoff that has disrupted global oil shipments.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish Court Annuls Main Opposition CHP Congress in Major Political Blow
item.description: An appeals court ruled that the Republican Peoples Party 2023 congress is legally invalid, dealing a significant setback to Turkeys main opposition party and deepening the countrys political uncertainty.

item.title: 🇪🇪 NATO Jet Downs Stray Ukrainian Drone Over Estonia in First Such Incident
item.description: A NATO fighter jet shot down a suspected stray Ukrainian drone over Estonia, marking the first such incident. Kyiv accused Moscow of intentionally redirecting Ukrainian drones into NATO territory.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Putins Attack on Hungarians in Ukraine Marks New Phase of War
item.description: Russian missiles struck Zakarpattia just 12 hours after Hungarys new prime minister took office, marking what observers call a new phase in the conflict targeting ethnic Hungarian communities in western Ukraine.

item.title: 📉 Euro Zone Growth Forecast Cut as Iran War Fuels Inflation
item.description: The European Commission slashed its 2026 growth forecast for the euro zone, warning that the Middle East conflict has triggered the second energy shock in under five years.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon Economy to Shrink 7% This Year as War With Israel Rages
item.description: Lebanon's finance minister warned the ongoing conflict with Hezbollah could cost the country $20 billion and shrink GDP by at least 7% this year.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Exports Rise 14.8% Despite Iran War Supply Disruptions
item.description: Japanese exports beat expectations with an eighth consecutive monthly increase, showing resilience despite major supply chain disruptions from the US-Israel-Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇫🇷 French Economy Contracts at Fastest Pace in 5 Years
item.description: France's private sector shrank at its steepest rate in five-and-a-half years in May, with services and manufacturing both declining as the Iran war energy shock hit Europe hard.

item.title: 🇵🇭 US, Philippines Near Deal on Economic Security Zone to Counter China
item.description: A senior US official said Washington expects to reach agreement soon on a long-term framework for an economic security zone with the Philippines amid rising tensions with Beijing.

item.title: 🛢️ Full Hormuz Oil Flows Unlikely Before 2027, UAE Oil Chief Says
item.description: The head of UAE state oil firm ADNOC warned that even if the Middle East conflict ended now, full oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz would not return until Q1-Q2 2027 due to infrastructure damage.
